findings.title: SSO Integration is Critical for Enterprise Onboarding
findings.description: Enterprise prospects require SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC for onboarding, as highlighted by multiple sources. Without this feature, procurement is blocked, indicating a critical need for integration with identity providers like Okta and Azure AD.
assumptions: Enterprise customers prioritize security and compliance in their procurement process.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise prospects require SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC before signing contracts.
"We can't deploy a SaaS tool that doesn't support SAML — it's a non-starter for our security team."

recommendations: Prioritize the development of SSO/SAML integration to facilitate enterprise onboarding.

findings.title: Need for Enhanced Data Isolation for Compliance
findings.description: Enterprise customers are concerned about data isolation and compliance, particularly regarding SOC 2 Type II requirements. Current reliance on application-level row filtering is insufficient, necessitating a review of data isolation methods.
assumptions: Enterprise customers will not proceed without adequate data isolation measures.

evidence.excerpt: Security audit flagged that tenant data isolation relies on application-level row filtering.
Kenji requires data residency in the Tokyo region for regulatory compliance.

recommendations: Implement proper data isolation boundaries to meet compliance requirements.

findings.title: Demand for Fine-Grained Role-Based Access Control
findings.description: Mid-market customers express a need for more granular role-based access control (RBAC) features. The current model is too simplistic, leading to inefficiencies and security concerns.
assumptions: Customers require customizable access controls to manage user permissions effectively.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers need fine-grained RBAC beyond our current admin/member/viewer model.
"The permissions model is too simple — I have to give everyone admin just to let them manage their own projects."

recommendations: Develop and implement a more flexible RBAC system with custom roles and permissions.

findings.title: Critical Need for Data Residency Controls
findings.description: Enterprise customers, particularly in regulated markets, require data residency controls to comply with local laws. This is a significant barrier to adoption for prospects in regions like the EU and APAC.
assumptions: Compliance with local data residency laws is essential for enterprise customers.

evidence.excerpt: GDPR and data sovereignty requirements mean enterprise customers must choose where their data is stored.
"Japanese financial services regulations require all customer data to remain within Japan."

recommendations: Implement region-pinned storage options to meet data residency requirements.

findings.title: Unmet Need for White-Labeling Features
findings.description: Several enterprise prospects have expressed a need for white-label branding capabilities, indicating a gap in the current offering that could hinder customer acquisition.
assumptions: Enterprise customers prefer customizable branding options to align with their corporate identity.

evidence.excerpt: Three enterprise prospects require white-label branding with custom logos, color schemes, and domain names.

recommendations: Explore the feasibility of adding white-label theming and custom domain support.

summary: The analysis reveals critical insights into enterprise customer needs, particularly around SSO integration, data isolation, and role-based access control. There is a strong demand for compliance with local regulations and customization options, indicating significant opportunity areas for product development.
